openmeter/progressmanager/adapter.purpose: Redis-backed implementation of progressmanager.Adapter storing/retrieving TTL-bound progress data as JSON; also provides adapterNoop (silent no-op) and MockProgressManager (testify mock for Service) in the same package.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: New() calls config.Validate() first and returns (Adapter, error). Required: non-nil Redis client, non-nil Logger, Expiration > 0.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (progressmanager.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Both adapter and adapterNoop assert interface compliance via blank-identifier assignment at package level.

patterns.name: Namespaced Redis key via getKey()
patterns.description: All Redis operations route through getKey(id) producing '<keyPrefix>:progress:<namespace>:<id>' (or without prefix when empty). Never construct keys inline.

patterns.name: redis.Nil mapped to GenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: When redis.Get returns redis.Nil, return models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...) so the HTTP layer maps it to 404 — not the raw redis error.
patterns.example: if cmd.Err() == redis.Nil { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...) }

patterns.name: Input validation before Redis operations
patterns.description: Both GetProgress and UpsertProgress call input.Validate() and wrap the error before any Redis call.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("validate get progress input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Noop asymmetry: writes succeed, reads return not-found
patterns.description: adapterNoop.UpsertProgress returns nil (writes discarded silently); adapterNoop.GetProgress returns GenericNotFoundError — callers treat noop writes as safe to ignore.

patterns.name: Mock implements Service not Adapter
patterns.description: MockProgressManager satisfies progressmanager.Service (including DeleteProgressByRuntimeID), not Adapter; keep in sync when Service gains methods.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New() constructor, adapter and adapterNoop type definitions — the wiring entry point for app/common.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.Validate() must reject nil Redis client and zero Expiration — never relax these guards.

key_file_guides.role: GetProgress and UpsertProgress using Redis GET/SET with JSON marshaling and TTL.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getKey() is the sole key-format authority; changing the format orphans all existing in-flight Redis records.

key_file_guides.role: No-op adapter; GetProgress always returns not-found, UpsertProgress is silent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not make UpsertProgress return a non-nil error — callers rely on noop writes being safe no-ops.

key_file_guides.role: Testify mock for progressmanager.Service used in unit tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mock is for Service (not Adapter); update it when the Service interface adds methods.

anti_patterns: Storing progress keys without namespace prefix — breaks multi-tenant isolation
Returning raw redis errors instead of GenericNotFoundError for redis.Nil — breaks HTTP 404 mapping
Calling Redis operations without first calling input.Validate()
Editing the key format in getKey() without a migration plan — orphans in-flight progress records

decisions.decision: Redis (not PostgreSQL/Ent) as the backing store
decisions.rationale: Progress data is transient and TTL-bound; Redis SET with expiration avoids schema migrations and garbage-collects stale progress without a cleanup job.

decisions.decision: Noop adapter co-located with the real adapter in the same package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the disabled-feature code path trivially discoverable, following the project pattern of noop implementations per optional feature.

code_examples.scenario: Wire the Redis adapter in app/common

openmeter/progressmanager/entity.purpose: Pure domain types for the progressmanager domain: ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput. No persistence logic — every type implements Validate() with errors.Join for multi-error aggregation.

patterns.name: Validate() collects all errors via errors.Join
patterns.description: Each Validate() appends all failures into a []error and returns errors.Join(...). Never return early on first error.
patterns.example: var errs []error; if a.ID == "" { errs = append(errs, errors.New("id is required")) }; return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Delegate validation to embedded types
patterns.description: Composite types call Validate() on embedded structs first and wrap with fmt.Errorf for context before appending.
patterns.example: if err := a.ProgressID.Validate(); err != nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("progress id: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: Business invariants in Progress.Validate()
patterns.description: Progress.Validate() enforces Success+Failed <= Total, non-zero counts require non-zero Total, and UpdatedAt must not be zero.
patterns.example: if a.Success+a.Failed > a.Total { errs = append(errs, errors.New("success and failed must be less than or equal to total")) }

patterns.name: Input types embed domain types directly
patterns.description: GetProgressInput embeds ProgressID; UpsertProgressInput embeds Progress — thin validation wrappers with no extra fields.

key_file_guides.role: Single file with all entity types (ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput) — source of truth for domain invariants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields to Progress requires updating Validate() to enforce new invariants; JSON tags must match the generated API schema.

anti_patterns: Returning on first validation error instead of collecting all errors with errors.Join
Adding persistence, Redis, or HTTP logic to entity types — this package is pure domain types
Omitting the UpdatedAt zero-check in Progress.Validate()
Embedding adapter-specific fields into entity types

decisions.decision: Entity types in a separate sub-package (entity/) rather than the root progressmanager package
decisions.rationale: Avoids import cycles: adapter and httpdriver both need the entity types but neither should import the other; a shared entity sub-package breaks the cycle.

openmeter/progressmanager/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the progressmanager domain, implementing a single GetProgress endpoint via the httptransport.Handler pipeline with namespace resolution from request context.

patterns.name: Handler interface composes sub-interfaces
patterns.description: The top-level Handler embeds ProgressHandler (exposing GetProgress()). New endpoint groups add a new sub-interface and embed it.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ProgressHandler }; type ProgressHandler interface { GetProgress() GetProgressHandler }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) ensures the concrete struct satisfies the Handler interface.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: Handlers call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) reading namespace from request context; returns HTTP 500 if missing — intentional for self-hosted single-namespace deployments.
patterns.example: ns, ok := h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); if !ok { return "", commonhttp.NewHTTPError(http.StatusInternalServerError, errors.New("internal server error")) }

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints with URL path args use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs[Req, Resp, Arg]; the first func decodes (ctx, *http.Request, arg)->(Req, error), the second is the operation.

patterns.name: Response type aliased from generated api package
patterns.description: GetProgressResponse is aliased to api.Progress (generated OpenAPI type); conversion from domain is via the pure progressToAPI() helper.
patterns.example: type GetProgressResponse = api.Progress; func progressToAPI(p entity.Progress) api.Progress { ... }

patterns.name: HandlerOption propagation via AppendOptions
patterns.description: Handler-level options propagate to each endpoint via httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("...")).

key_file_guides.role: Handler/ProgressHandler interfaces, handler struct, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveNamespace returns HTTP 500 (not 401/403) when namespace is missing — intentional for StaticNamespaceDecoder in self-hosted deployments.

key_file_guides.role: GetProgress endpoint: decode, operation (calls service.GetProgress), encode, and progressToAPI() conversion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A nil progress after a successful service call is treated as an internal error (not not-found) — the nil guard prevents silent data loss.

anti_patterns: Calling the domain service from the decoder function — decode must only extract request fields
Returning domain entity types directly as HTTP responses — always convert via progressToAPI() using api types
Hardcoding the namespace string instead of calling h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Adding business logic to progressToAPI() — it must be a pure field mapping

decisions.decision: GetProgressResponse aliased to api.Progress rather than a new struct
decisions.rationale: The generated api.Progress is the contract-stable OpenAPI type; aliasing avoids a redundant intermediate struct and ensures the response matches the spec.

code_examples.scenario: Register the GetProgress handler in the v1 router
